place: Finderne CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: The Van Veghten House, built in 1725, was the headquarters of Quartermaster General Nathanael Greene during the 1778–79 American Revolutionary War encampment.
Finderne is home to the Middlebrook Crossing industrial park, the Promenade shopping center, and TD Bank Ballpark, the Somerset Patriots’ home field.
Located between Bound Brook and Somerville, Finderne stretches from older Foothill Road neighborhoods to newer developments along the Raritan River.
The Van Veghten House was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1979, noted as one of the few remaining Raritan River mansions.
Finderne’s geographic coordinates are 40.5632 degrees north latitude and 74.5777 degrees west longitude in Somerset County.
Finderne is a census-designated place, meaning it has no governmental functions and is not a legal entity.
